body.title: Graph-tree embeddings + teach-on-miss (LLM grows the graph, never answers); retire nomic entirely
body.text:
# D-graph-tree-embeddings-teach-on-miss-2026-06-30
**title:** Graph-tree embeddings + teach-on-miss; retire nomic entirely
**date:** 2026-06-30
**status:** open

## Decision narrative
KEYSTONE compiled-inference design, confirmed with Akien 2026-06-30. The LLM NEVER produces the answer; it only PUSHES NODES into the graph tree, and the graph tree always produces output in its own node-activation shape. First application: embeddings = the SET of activated word-graph concept-nodes; LIKE/similarity = node-overlap, NOT cosine. Nomic dense vectors retire ENTIRELY; all consumers (valence anchors, kNN, compression, distillation, cortex) redefine in node-overlap. On a MISS the LLM pushes missing nodes WITH a checkable target, committed only if the graph then produces it (proof-on-write, the graph-write analogue of proof-on-close). teach-on-miss SUPERSEDES the earlier 'LLM-compare-for-training'. Node MERGE required (living-entities discipline). Convergence signal: teach-call-rate declines over time. Affordable now (free Ollama Cloud / Hex). Full principle in architecture/cc.0.graph-embeddings-teach-on-miss.*. This is a DESIGN-THEN-BUILD epic: define-representation + teach-on-miss need a CC design pass first; the rest are specified Haiku-buildable (dogfood of the dev-pipeline). Advisor added the backfill ticket (else nomic deletion silently strands LIKE over existing memories) and flagged closing the superseded T-igor-embedder-via-rack (done).

## Hypothesis
After this ships, igor's embeddings are node-activation sets with LIKE=node-overlap, the LLM only TEACHES the graph on misses (never answers), nomic is fully retired, and the LLM-teach-call-rate DECLINES over time.

## Measurement Signal
teach-call-rate time series declines as the graph fills; grep nomic / embedder.py / memory_embeddings -> 0 live refs; LIKE retrieval ranks similar memories via node-overlap; proof-on-write accepts good node-pushes and rejects bad ones (both paths tested).

## Intention
I intend that cognition runs in graph-trees with the LLM only growing the graph (never answering), so the system compiles inference and learns its way off the LLM.

## Intention mapping (vs extant)
ADVANCES the compiled-inference intention ([[project_compiled_inference_vision]], [[project_intention_compiler_reflexive]], [[project_build_packet_pre_inference_compiler]]) — teach-on-miss is the concrete mechanism for 'move capability out of the LLM, one piece at a time'. The node-merge requirement DECOMPOSES from [[project_intentions_as_living_entities]] (compete/merge/decompose) applied at the concept-node level. Does not conflict with any open decision.
